frameworks.name: complex networks (visibility graph)
frameworks.one_line: EEG time series mapped to graphs whose topology indexes emotional state

frameworks.name: network entropy measures
frameworks.one_line: nodal degree entropy and clustering coefficient entropy as discriminative features

frameworks.name: support vector machine classification
frameworks.one_line: supervised classifier consuming the network-entropy features

core_question: Can visibility-graph-derived network entropy of EEG support subject-independent emotion recognition?
key_assumptions: EEG signals carry spatial-topological and temporal structure that maps to emotion
visibility graphs preserve emotion-relevant dynamics of the EEG time series
channel relevance can be ranked by AUC for feature selection
manipulations: two- vs three-class emotion labelling (positive/negative vs positive/negative/neutral)
channel subset selection (62 channels reduced to AUC-significant 18)
type of network-entropy feature (nodal degree entropy vs clustering coefficient entropy)
key_findings: AUC selected 18 of 62 channels as significant for emotion discrimination
average across-subject accuracy of 87.26% for positive vs negative
average across-subject accuracy of 68.44% for positive vs negative vs neutral
mean accuracy improved by about 2.28% over prior methods
interpretations: fused network entropy on visibility graphs captures emotion-relevant EEG structure usable across subjects
complex-network features can mitigate individual differences in physiological responses
